levels.context: st evening, we trapped below 6735, recovered, and ripped to 6808 high of day. Tomorrow, however, is FOMC. Will it spoil this

supports.context: st evening, we trapped below 6735, recovered, and ripped to 6808 high of day. Tomorrow, however, is FOMC. Will it spoil this

highlights.text: It is also why I wrote last Wednesday at 4pm: “Bear Case Thursday: Begins on the failure of 6819”.

I wrote on Friday at 4pm: “My general lean is ES can try and recover 6689 to generate a rally to 6743, 6754, 6766, then 6819” We got started right on this after 6pm Sunday and by 7pm we recovered 6689 and ran to 6743+ yesterday, exactly per plan.
I wrote yesterday at 4pm: “ The bull case for tomorrow is that ES can keep up the momentum from last evenings 6689-90 Failed Breakdown, and run to backtest 6819.
I discussed entries for this leg above, but in a strong bull case, ES will hold 6743 (or quick trap below 6735) then head up direct.” We got right to work here.
Thursday morning, price picked its path, and down we went going elevator down .
Every elevator down sell sees an equally violent squeeze the other way.
Those squeezes however, only occur when price triggers my core setup: The Failed Breakdown which occurs when price flushes a major low, traps shorts, then reverses.
1) To start a squeeze, they’d need to put in a Failed Breakdown and to do this, theyd need to recover 6689 which was a big shelf all day Friday.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow

post_title: FOMC Tomorrow. Can The New SPX Rally Survive? March 18 Plan
